what_it_does: This file is a ledger attachment record that links a channel-memory entry to an image asset located at image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-39.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
The attachment_type is 'images' and the target uses an image:// URI scheme
No code symbols or executable content is present
standouts: References a specific image asset via a URI-like target
Serves purely as metadata linking a channel to an external image
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a consistent ledger attachment schema
Includes a unique id, channel name, attachment type, target reference, and ISO timestamp
concerns: Target URI scheme and resolution mechanism are not defined in this file
No provenance fields such as added_by, caption, or mime type are present
notes: A minimal channel-memory attachment record pointing a design-source channel entry to an image in recent-pitches.
questions: What does the image at p-39 depict?
Who added this attachment and for what reason?
Does 'images' imply multiple images or a single image asset?
How is the image:// target resolved or validated?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to associate a recent pitch image with the design-source channel so the image can be retrieved alongside channel memory.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI target scheme to reference a design-source asset.
contribution: Provides a timestamped attachment reference that connects channel memory to an image asset, supporting retrieval and traceability of associated media.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter ledger attachment record with id att-7f9c3fa7 linking the design-source channel to image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-39, added on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record an image attachment for the design-source channel memory.
